{{Quote|The Rianitus period ''[…]'' is notable for the 275-year reign of Blotus the Hutt, who defied traditional Hutt stereotypes by becoming one of the most distinguished personages to ever hold the chancellor's office.|Voren Na'al|<ref name="NEC" />}}
'''Blotus''' was a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] for a 275-[[Standard year/Legends|year]] term during the [[Rianitus Period]], an [[Time/Legends|era]] that lasted from [[9000 BBY|9000]] to [[8000 BBY]]. Formerly an [[Administrator/Legends|administrator]] of several [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Planet/Legends|planets]] that had separated from the [[Hutt Empire]], Blotus joined the Republic and quickly became a popular figure within its [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]]. As Chancellor, he overcame the stereotypes assigned to members of his [[Species/Legends|species]] and ushered in a new age of prosperity for the Republic. After his [[Death/Legends|death]], Blotus was remembered as one of the most prominent beings to serve as Supreme Chancellor in [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] history.

==Biography==
Blotus the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] lived during the [[Rianitus Period]], an era of [[Time/Legends|time]] that spanned from [[9000 BBY|9000]] to [[8000 BBY]].<ref name="NEC" /> He served as [[Administrator/Legends|administrator]] of a string of [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] that had broken away from what remained of the [[Hutt Empire]] and applied for membership in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]],<ref name="Codex" /> the dominant government in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="NEC" /> Though tensions existed between the Republic and the Hutt [[Species/Legends|species]], Blotus and his planets were accepted into the government's fold, and the Hutt became a popular being within the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]].<ref name="Codex" />

Some time later, Blotus became [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] of the Republic, having been elected to the position by a substantial majority.<ref name="Codex" /> Blotus's ascension to the [[Office of the Supreme Chancellor/Legends|office]] marked the apex of millennia of Hutt immigration to the Republic; in becoming the first Hutt Supreme Chancellor,<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Blotus had dispelled the fixed stereotypes assigned to the Hutt species.<ref name="NEC" /> Under his reign as Chancellor, the Republic benefited from a period of prosperity. When Blotus granted tax breaks to independent [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]], trade prospered within the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], and the Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] specifically enjoyed substantial economic growth.<ref name="Codex" />

The Hutt's term as the leader of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government lasted for 275 [[Standard year/Legends|years]],<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|80|Blotus the Hutt}}</ref> after which Blotus [[Death/Legends|died]] at the age of 912, peacefully passing away in his sleep. By the time of the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] between the Republic and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]], Blotus's Chancellorship was remembered with affection, so much that the Hutt's favorite seat in the lounge of the [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Building]] was purposely left vacant.<ref name="Codex" /> Later generations regarded Blotus the Hutt as one of the most illustrious figures to serve as Chancellor in the history of the Republic, a claim supported by [[Historian/Legends|historian]] and [[Author/Legends|author]] [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]] in [[36 ABY]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Personality and traits==
A male Hutt, Blotus was a former administrator of multiple worlds, and was regarded as a competent individual.<ref name="Codex" /> Blotus overcame the long-established stereotypes associated with Hutts to become the Republic's Supreme Chancellor, and, centuries after his death, he was remembered as one of the most renowned beings in galactic history to hold the office of Chancellor.<ref name="NEC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Blotus the Hutt was first mentioned in ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', a second-generation [[Star Wars: Essential Guides|Essential Guide]] written by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and published on [[October 25]], [[2005]].<ref name="NEC" /> Though Blotus did not appear in the [[2011]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', he was given an entry in the game's Codex databank, which provided new information.<ref name="Codex" />
